title: Dart Object-Oriented Programming Mock Test
description: Master Dart OOP concepts including classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, abstraction, mixins, and interfaces

questions.text: What keyword is used to create a class in Dart?
questions.explanation: The 'class' keyword is used to define a class in Dart. Classes are blueprints for creating objects.
hints: Same as many other OOP languages

questions.text: How do you create an instance of a class in Dart?
options: new MyClass() (both B and C work)
questions.explanation: Both 'MyClass()' and 'new MyClass()' create instances. The 'new' keyword is optional in modern Dart.

questions.text: What is a constructor in Dart?
options: A method that destroys an object
A special method that initializes an object when created
A method that copies an object
questions.explanation: A constructor is a special method that runs when an object is instantiated, initializing the object's state.
The constructor Person(this.name) automatically assigns the parameter to the instance variable. Output is 'John'.

questions.text: Which keyword is used for inheritance in Dart?
questions.explanation: The 'extends' keyword is used for inheritance, allowing a class to inherit properties and methods from a parent class.

questions.text: What is method overriding in Dart?
options: Creating multiple methods with same name
Redefining a parent class method in a child class
Hiding a method from outside access
questions.explanation: Method overriding allows a child class to provide a specific implementation of a method already defined in its parent class.

options: Calls a method from the current class
questions.explanation: 'super' refers to the parent class and is used to call parent class methods or constructors.

questions.text: What is an abstract class in Dart?
options: A class that cannot be instantiated
A class that can only have static methods
A class with only private members
A class that is automatically final
questions.explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly. They provide a base class with abstract methods that subclasses must implement.

questions.text: What is the difference between 'extends' and 'implements'?
options: extends inherits implementation, implements only inherits interface
implements inherits implementation, extends only inherits interface
extends is for classes, implements for interfaces only
questions.explanation: extends inherits both interface and implementation. implements only inherits the interface, requiring reimplementation of all methods.

questions.text: What is a mixin in Dart?
options: A way to merge two classes
A way to reuse code across multiple class hierarchies
questions.explanation: Mixins allow code reuse across multiple class hierarchies without multiple inheritance. Use 'with' keyword to apply mixins.
Encapsulation bundles data and methods, hiding internal state. In Dart, prefixing with '_' makes members private to the library.

questions.text: How do you make a variable private in Dart?
questions.explanation: In Dart, prefixing an identifier with '_' makes it private to its library (file). There is no 'private' keyword.

options: A constructor that doesn't always create a new instance
A constructor that runs in a separate thread
A constructor that can only be called once
questions.explanation: Factory constructors can return existing instances (from cache), return subtypes, or perform complex logic. They don't always create new objects.

questions.text: What is a static member in Dart?
options: A member that belongs to the class, not instances
A member that cannot be changed
A member that is automatically inherited
questions.explanation: Static members belong to the class itself rather than instances. They are accessed using the class name, not an object reference.
